configfile — Configuration file tools¶

New in version 0.1.

Module author: Claude Gibert <claude.gibert@synopticview.co.uk>

The ConfigFile class¶

class core.configfile.ConfigFile(nocasedict)¶

The ConfigFile class uses the Python’s ConfigParser class to read configuration files. A ConfigFile object is a dictionary with entries of different types, including other dictionaries. A few goodies have been added:

  • the keys of the dictionaries are case insensitive, e.g. FileName = filename
  • items between commas on the right hand side in the configuration file are converted to list, unless the object is created with the option process_coma=False
  • if an item on the right hand side is written [name], then the section [name] if nested at that level.
  • a special section called [__types__] can define the type of the right hand side values.
  • a ConfigFile object can be printed in a neat way.

If you consider the following configuration file (config.def):

[servers]
main = [dartagnan]
backup = [aramis]

[dartagnan]
hostname = zx56
port = 3244
credentials = read, write
since = 20091225

[aramis]
hostname = zxu167
port = 3256
credentials = read
since = 20081114

[__types__]
since = Date
port = int

and the following code:

from semperpy.core.date import Date
from semperpy.core.configfile import ConfigFile

f = ConfigFile('config.def')
print f
servers:
        main:
                credentials:
                        read
                         write
                hostname:
                        zx56
                port:
                        3244
                since:
                        2009-12-25
        backup:
                credentials:
                        read
                hostname:
                        zxu167
                port:
                        3256
                since:
                        2008-11-14
aramis:
        credentials:
                read
        hostname:
                zxu167
        port:
                3256
        since:
                2008-11-14
dartagnan:
        credentials:
                read
                 write
        hostname:
                zx56
        port:
                3244
        since:
                2009-12-25

print type(f['servers']['Main']['port'])
<type 'int'>

Please note that nested sections are inserted as sub-dictionaries. The dates have been allocated from the class Date as the output is formatted. The type of the port entry is int as required, and keys are case insensitive.

ConfigFile(filename, process_coma = False, overwrite = True)¶
Parameters:
  • filename (string or list of strings) – full path to the configuration file.
  • process_coma (bool) – controls the interpretation of comas on the right-hand side. By default it is True, a list is created.
  • overwrite (bool) – if a list of files is provided, files are read backwards from the list, if overwrite is True, common sections are overwritten so that the first file has precedence.
